observations.recommendation:
In relation thereof, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an inventory committee that will facilitate the conduct of the physical
count of its PPE as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated
January 31, 2020. Also, to instruct the following in relation thereto, to
wit:
a. Inventory Committee to prepare the RPCPPE, in compliance
with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
b. General Services Officer to maintain and update her PCs per
category of PPE, pursuant to Section 119 of MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I;
c. Municipal Accountant to maintain and update her PPELC per
category of PPE, pursuant to Section 120 of MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I; and
d. Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal
Accountant to reconcile their PCs and PPELCs, respectively.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended to the Municipal Mayor to
instruct the General Services Office to prepare and submit the SSMI
for CY 2022, in compliance with Section 121 of the MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
a.) The Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer
on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December each year, an assessment
roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been newly
assessed or reassessed and the values of such properties;
b.) The Municipal Treasurer furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified
list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for
the current year; and
c.) The Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if
applicable.

We recommended to the LCE to intensify the collection efforts
earnestly by the Municipal Treasurer, designated collecting officers
and sub-collectors to maximize the receipt of local fees and taxes
accruing to the Municipality, pursuant to Section 17 of R.A. No. 7160,
otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991”.

As a result thereof, we recommended that the Municipality procure
directly to PS-DBM for the acquisition of common-use supplies, and if
the same were not available, then secure a certification for the non-
availability of stocks from the said office.

In relation the foregoing, we recommended that the BAC prepare the
PMR with the approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity and submit
the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format within fourteen
(14) calendar days after the end of each semester and the APP to GPPB
on or before end of January of the budget year.

6. In relation to the foregoing, we recommend that the Municipality shall submit the copy of all contracts entered by the Management within five (5) working days upon perfection to the Office of the Auditor for review and evaluation, pursuantto Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009- 001 dated February 12, 2009.

Hence, we highly recommend that the Municipal Mayor to create an
Inventory Committee which shall compose of adequate number of
members who shall be temporarily relieved of all their regular duties
to devote their full time to be able to complete the physical inventory in
three months or less, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020. After which, it is recommended that the
Municipal Mayor to direct the:

a. Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan and
accordingly conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all
PPE of the Municipality and prepare the corresponding Report
on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
a (RPCPPE) following the guidelines set forth in said COA
a Circular;

b. General Services Officer to update the GSO’s property records
based on the RPCPPE; and

c. General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile
their respective property records to come up with the reconciled
balances of PPE accounts to be considered as the correct balance
of the PPE.

As a result, we recommend to intensify the collection efforts earnestly
by the Municipal Treasurer, designated collecting officers and sub-
collectors to maximize the receipt of local fees and taxes accruing to the
Municipality, in compliance with Section 17 of Republic Act No. 7160,
otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991”.

Thus, we recommend that he Municipality shall procure directly to PS-
DBM for the acquisition of common-use supplies, and if the same were
not available, then secure a certification for the non-availability of
stocks from the said office.

In relation to the foregoing, we recommend that the Municipality shall
submit the copy of all contracts entered by the Management within five
(5) working days upon perfection to the Office of the Auditor for review
and evaluation, pursuant to Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of the Commission
on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

a. To conduct physical inventory of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) for CY 2020, pursuant to Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992; b. Prepare a report thereon to be submitted by the Inventory Committee to the Audit Team and the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with that of the accounting records; and c. Make the necessary adjustments thereon.

a. To conduct of physical inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)

b. Prepare a report thereon to be submitted by the Inventory Committee

c. Make the necessary adjustments thereon.

a. Reconcile the Bank Statements with the Bank Ledgers.
b. Prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement in accordance with Section 3.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
c. Distribute the five (5) copies to the different person concerned as stated in the above circulars.

observations.managementAction: The audit team informed the Municipal Accountant regarding the submission of the Bank monthly Statements with the Bank Ledgers. The management committee by next year that its preparation and submission would be made in accordance with above COA Circular.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to management to conduct the yearly inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment, and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE), pursuant to Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) For Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management gave assurance that they would conduct the yearly inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) pursuant to Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) For Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
observations.reason: Due to COVID-19 Pandemic, the scheduled physical count of PPEs was postponed. However, they will conduct the same within the 1st semester of 2021.

observations.recommendation: Furnish the copy of all contracts entered by the Management within five (5) working days upon perfection to the Office of the Auditor for review and evaluation, pursuant to Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
observations.managementAction: We were assured by the management that they would comply with the submission of government contracts in the beginning this coming fiscal year.
observations.reason: Quarantine protocol and series of lockdown among boarders prevented the required submission of the contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management should submit the Paid Disbursement Vouchers related to Calendar Year 2019 totaling to One Million Pesos (P1,000,000.00), pursuant to Section 100 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) 1445, known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: Much of our desire to submit on time the Paid Disbursement Vouchers related to Calendar Year 2019 totaling to One Million Pesos (P1,000,000.00) pursuant to Section 100 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) 1445, we were not able to submit on time due to the COVID-19 pandemic and government lockdowns that prevented submission.
observations.reason: Due to COVID-19 pandemic, we were prevented by the government lockdown and the pandemic itself which prevented us to submit on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the management to initiate the necessary conduct of physical inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment for CY 2018 and the Office of the General Services also to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and reconciliation of its records with that of the accounting office.
observations.reason: Due to skeletal manpowe r and lack of preparedn ess

observations.recommendation:
We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Accountant the following:
a. Reconcile the Bank Statements with the General Ledgers.
b. Prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statement in accordance with Section 3.2 of
the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
c. Distribute the five (5) copies to the different person concerned as stated in the
above circulars.
d. Submit to the audit team the Bank Reconciliation Statement pursuant to
Section 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2, 1996.

We recommend to the management to create an Inventory Team that would
conduct the yearly physical inventory of the Property, Plant and Equipment, submit
the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE),
pursuant to Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386
dated October 20, 1992 and Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System (MNGAS) For Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I,
respectively.

We recommend the management to submit the contracts on the procurement
of infrastructure projects, and goods together with the required supporting
documents relative to the competitive public buildings, pursuant to Section 3.1.1
and 3.1.2 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-001 dated February
12, 2009.

We recommend that the management should submit the Paid Disbursement
Vouchers related to Calendar Year 2019 totaling to One Million Pesos
(P1,000,000.00), pursuant to Section 100 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) 1445,
otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to prepare the needed adjusting journal entry.

We recommend to the Municipal Accountant to prepare the needed adjusting journal
entry.

We recommend to the management to initiate the necessary conduct of physical
inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment for CY 2018 and the Office of the General
Services also to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and
reconciliation of its records with that of the accounting office.
We recommend to the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary Adjusting Journal Entry (JEV) and to take up in the books of accounts

We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, and for public bidding, if any, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, 18 minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We recommend to the management to initiate the necessary conduct of physical inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment for CY 2017 and the Office of the General Services also to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and reconciliation of its records with that of the accounting office.

Require the Municipal General Services Office to take the lead in the conduct of physical inventory of all its properties and 

prepare the Report on the Physical Count of 
PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records.
We recommend to the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary Adjusting Journal Entry (JEV) and to take up in the books of accounts

We recommend that the procuring entity should submit a copy of the contracts and purchase orders within five days from issuance as required under COA Circular 2009-001, and for public bidding, if any, together with the bidding documents, BAC resolution, minutes of the proceedings, abstracts, and such other documents peculiar to the contract and/or to the mode of procurement which are deemed necessary in the conduct of auditorial review and in the technical evaluation thereof.

We recommend to the management to initiate the necessary conduct of physical inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment for CY 2017 and the Office of the General Services also to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and  reconciliation of its records with that of the accounting office.

1. We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer be instructed to prepare the necessary bank reconciliation statement as well the submission of bank statements to the Audit Team for  CY 2016 and ensuing years together with the JEVs.

Prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation for each bank account.

Management committed to prepare regularly bank reconciliation statements.

3. We recommend to the management to properly allocate in accordance with regulations  such as 20% Development Fund, 5% GAD, Calamity Fund, etc. the amount that may be received for the ensuing years except those amount with specific instruction for its utilization.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer be instructed to prepare the necessary bank reconciliation statement as well the submission of bank statements to the Audit Team for  CY 2016 and ensuing years together with the JEVs.
Prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation for each bank account.

Require the Municipal General Services Office to take the lead in the conduct of physical inventory of all its properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and to coordinate with the Accounting Office for the reconciliation of the records.

3.1 We recommend to the management to properly allocate in accordance with regulations  such as 20% Development Fund, 5% GAD, Calamity Fund, etc. the amount that may be received for the ensuing years except those amount with specific instruction for its utilization.

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately to the Audit Team the Disbursements Vouchers and RCI of all funds, together with the complete supporting documents for the period January to December 2015.

Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to submit the financial statements and other accounting reports for the CY 2015. The prompt and regular submission of these reports must be given special attention and be acted upon the soonest by the responsible officials concerned. Henceforth, these reports must be submitted regularly.
